title: Island Records Is First Label in Decades to Land Back-to-Back Best New Artist Grammy Wins
text: Olivia Dean won the Grammy Award for Best New Artist at the 68th annual Grammy Awards ceremony on February 1, 2026. Her victory marks a significant milestone for Island Records, as it follows Chappell Roan's win in the same category the previous year. Island Records is now the first record label in at least 40 years to land back-to-back best new artist victories. This accomplishment places Island among only three labels ever to achieve consecutive wins in this category since the award's creation in 1960. The previous labels to accomplish this feat were Warner Bros. Records (with winners in 1980-1981) and Epic Records (with three consecutive wins in the mid-1980s). Island Records is part of the larger REPUBLIC Collective, which encompasses multiple imprints including Republic Records, Mercury Records, and Def Jam.
